pattern: Each extra student should go to the class with the largest marginal ratio gain, which a max-heap keyed on that gain delivers greedily.
hints: Adding a student to a class raises its ratio from pass/total to (pass+1)/(total+1); the gain shrinks as the class grows.
Because the marginal gain is largest for the class that benefits most right now, repeatedly serve that class.
Push each class onto a max-heap keyed by its marginal gain, pop the top, add a student, recompute its gain and push it back, repeating for every extra student; finally average all ratios.

edge_inputs.description: two extra students both help the first class most
four extra students spread across classes
a fully passing class gives zero gain so others are preferred
one extra student, pick the bigger improvement

stress.description: many classes with random pass<=total and a moderate extra budget
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(1, min(size, 100000))
    classes = []
    for _ in range(n):
        total = rng.randrange(1, 100001)
        passi = rng.randrange(1, total + 1)
        classes.append([passi, total])
    extra = rng.randrange(1, 2001)
    return [classes, extra]
